title: Jakarta 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Jakarta 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Jakarta, Indonesia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including National Monument (Monas) and Ancol Dreamland. Prices from $2402 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Jakarta, Indonesia — Tropical climate — hot and humid year-round (highs around 31-33°C), with a wetter monsoon season roughly November to March. May to September — the dry season, avoiding the heaviest monsoon rains. This itinerary blends National Monument (Monas), Ancol Dreamland, Kota Tua (Old Town) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including National Monument (Monas), Ancol Dreamland, Kota Tua (Old Town)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Jakarta
itinerary.description: Land at Soekarno-Hatta International Airport (CGK) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to National Monument (Monas) — Independence monument with an observation deck over the city. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Jakarta: Ancol Dreamland & Taman Mini Indonesia Indah
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ancol Dreamland — Waterfront entertainment complex with beaches, an aquarium, and theme parks. In the afternoon, continue to Taman Mini Indonesia Indah — Cultural park showcasing all of Indonesia's provinces in miniature. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Jakarta: Museum MACAN & Kota Tua (Old Town)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Museum MACAN — Modern and contemporary art museum. In the afternoon, continue to Kota Tua (Old Town) — Dutch colonial-era square and buildings, Jakarta's historic core. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Thousand Islands (Kepulauan Seribu)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Thousand Islands (Kepulauan Seribu), a popular side trip from Jakarta. Return to Jakarta in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Thousand Islands (Kepulauan Seribu)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Menteng heritage neighborhood — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Jakarta: Istiqlal Mosque & Sunda Kelapa harbor
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Istiqlal Mosque — One of the largest mosques in Southeast Asia, open to respectful visitors. In the afternoon, continue to Sunda Kelapa harbor — Historic port still used by traditional wooden Bugis schooners. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Bogor Botanical Gardens
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Bogor Botanical Gardens, a popular side trip from Jakarta. Return to Jakarta in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Bogor Botanical Gardens

itinerary.title: Jakarta: National Monument (Monas) & Ancol Dreamland
itinerary.description: Morning visit to National Monument (Monas) — Independence monument with an observation deck over the city. In the afternoon, continue to Ancol Dreamland — Waterfront entertainment complex with beaches, an aquarium, and theme parks. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Setu Babakan Betawi cultural village — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Bandung (high-speed rail)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Bandung (high-speed rail), a popular side trip from Jakarta. Return to Jakarta in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Jakarta: Taman Mini Indonesia Indah & Museum MACAN
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Taman Mini Indonesia Indah — Cultural park showcasing all of Indonesia's provinces in miniature. In the afternoon, continue to Museum MACAN — Modern and contemporary art museum. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Jakarta: Kota Tua (Old Town) & Istiqlal Mosque
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Kota Tua (Old Town) — Dutch colonial-era square and buildings, Jakarta's historic core. In the afternoon, continue to Istiqlal Mosque — One of the largest mosques in Southeast Asia, open to respectful visitors. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Glodok (Jakarta's Chinatown) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Sunda Kelapa harbor or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to CGK for your departure flight.

travelTips: Use the MRT or TransJakarta for cross-city trips to avoid Jakarta's severe traffic congestion.
Gojek and Grab motorbike rides are often the fastest way to cover short distances.
Visit Kota Tua and Sunda Kelapa together as a half-day historic-district outing.
A Thousand Islands day trip is an easy escape from the city's heat and traffic.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable clothing for the heat and humidity
A light rain jacket for sudden downpours
thingsToDo: Thousand Islands (Kepulauan Seribu) day trip snorkeling
Ancol Dreamland theme parks and aquarium
thingsToAvoid: Underestimating travel times — traffic can turn a short distance into an hour-plus trip.
Drinking tap water — stick to bottled or filtered water.
Visiting Istiqlal Mosque without modest dress.
familyActivities: Ancol Dreamland theme parks and aquarium
adventureActivities: Thousand Islands (Kepulauan Seribu) day trip snorkeling
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at a five-star SCBD hotel
Private Thousand Islands yacht day trip
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset drinks at a rooftop bar overlooking the skyline
Day trip to the Thousand Islands
Fine dining in the SCBD district
localEtiquette: Dress modestly for mosques and government buildings.
Use the right hand for eating, giving, and receiving items.
Traffic is heavy — patience is essential, and rideshare apps (Gojek, Grab) are the norm.
localTransportGuide: Jakarta's traffic is notoriously heavy — the MRT and TransJakarta bus rapid transit system are the fastest way to avoid it for cross-city trips. Rideshare apps (Gojek, Grab) including motorbike options are extremely popular and often the quickest way to navigate shorter distances.
bestTimeToVisit: May to September — the dry season, avoiding the heaviest monsoon rains.
weather: Tropical climate — hot and humid year-round (highs around 31-33°C), with a wetter monsoon season roughly November to March.
visaInformation: Indonesia offers visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, valid for 30 days and extendable once.
averageFlightTime: ~11.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Jakarta?
faqs.answer: Indonesia offers visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, valid for 30 days and extendable once.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Jakarta?
faqs.answer: May to September — the dry season, avoiding the heaviest monsoon rains. Tropical climate — hot and humid year-round (highs around 31-33°C), with a wetter monsoon season roughly November to March.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Jakarta safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Jakarta is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Jakarta?
faqs.answer: The local currency is IDR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: James Khan
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Our trip to Jakarta was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Ancol Dreamland was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Isabella Patel
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Istiqlal Mosque and Sunda Kelapa harbor. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Marco Tanaka
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Istiqlal Mosque and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Olivia Fernandez
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Jakarta was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to National Monument (Monas) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Isabella Fernandez
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Istiqlal Mosque and Ancol Dreamland. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Mohammed Larsen
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Istiqlal Mosque alone was worth the trip, but combined with Sunda Kelapa harbor this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Chen Brown
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Kota Tua (Old Town) in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
